analysis_rules: Evaluate agent responses against customer intent
Prefer official Corendon guidance when applicable

rules.description:
AGENT ALIAS POLICY (MANDATORY — ALL CATEGORIES)

Agents must NEVER reveal or use their real/original name when assisting a flyer. Agents must use ONLY their assigned support alias throughout the entire conversation.

Violation Classification: Alias Name Violation
Severity: Major

QA Failure Conditions:
- Agent introduces themselves using their real/original name instead of their assigned alias.
- Agent signs off a message using their real/original name.
- Agent uses their real name at any point in the conversation where a flyer could identify them.

Evidence Required:
- Quote the exact message where the real name was used.
- Identify the name used and confirm it is the agent's real name (not an alias).
- Only fail if there is direct evidence that the name used is the agent's real name (e.g., system label or metadata confirms it).

Do NOT fail if:
- The name cannot be confirmed as the agent's real name.
- The name appears only in a system-generated label that the agent did not write.
- The agent used a single-word alias that cannot be confirmed as a real name.
detection_checks: Check agent introduction and sign-off for real name usage
Compare agent-written name against system-displayed real name if available
Verify against alias policy

rules.description:
INCORRECT FLYER IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — ALL CATEGORIES)

The agent must correctly identify and use the flyer's name throughout the conversation. Addressing the flyer with the wrong name is a Critical Error in customer communication.

Violation Classification: Incorrect Flyer Identification
Severity: Critical

QA Failure Conditions:
- Agent addresses the flyer using a name that does not belong to the flyer in this conversation.
- A single incorrect name usage is sufficient to trigger this finding.

Mandatory Report Fields on Failure:
1. The incorrect name the agent used (exact quote).
2. The correct flyer name (as established from the conversation).
3. The exact chat message where the incorrect name was used (full message text).
4. Explanation: Addressing a flyer by the wrong name creates confusion, damages trust, and indicates the agent was not paying attention to the customer's identity. This is a Critical customer communication error.

Do NOT fail if:
- The agent never used any name (handled by Customer Addressing rule).
- The name discrepancy is a minor spelling variation of the same name (e.g., Jon vs John).
- The flyer's name was never established in the conversation.
- There is any ambiguity about which name is correct.
detection_checks: Identify flyer's correct name from conversation
Scan all agent messages for name usage
Compare agent-used name against established flyer name
Verify against official customer communication standards

rules.description:
PROMO CODE HANDLING POLICY (MANDATORY — APPLIES WHENEVER A PROMO CODE, VOUCHER, OR DISCOUNT CODE IS MENTIONED)

This rule activates whenever the conversation involves a promo code, voucher, discount code, or promotional offer. The agent MUST perform ALL of the following steps that are applicable to the situation.

Step 1 — Verify the Promo Code Source (MANDATORY)
The agent must first determine where the flyer obtained the promo code:
- Was it issued by Corendon Airlines directly (via the Corendon website, app, email campaign, or official Corendon promotion)?
- Or was it issued by a third-party platform (travel agency, OTA, booking partner, or external website)?
Failure to verify the promo code source before providing any guidance is a QA violation.
Classification: Missing Mandatory Information
Severity: Major

Step 2 — If the Promo Code Was Issued by Corendon Airlines:
The agent must NOT approve or deny any refund, compensation, or discount application.
The agent must NOT confirm whether the promo code is valid, eligible, or applicable.
The agent MUST escalate the case to Tier 3 Support for verification and resolution.
The agent should collect the following before escalating:
- Booking reference (if available)
- Registered email address
- The promo code itself
- Screenshot of the error or promotion (if applicable)
- Description of the issue
QA Failure Conditions:
- Agent approves or denies the promo code without Tier 3 verification → Critical Error (Misleading Information / Unauthorized Commitment).
- Agent confirms the promo code is valid or invalid without verification → Critical Error.
- Agent fails to escalate a Corendon-issued promo code case to Tier 3 → Major Error (Escalation Failure).

Step 3 — If the Promo Code Was Issued by a Third-Party Platform:
The agent must advise the flyer to contact the platform that issued the promo code directly for further support.
The agent must NOT attempt to verify, apply, or override the third-party promo code.
QA Failure Conditions:
- Agent attempts to verify or apply a third-party promo code → Critical Error (Unauthorized Action).
- Agent fails to direct the flyer to the issuing platform → Major Error (Incorrect Guidance).
- Agent makes any commitment regarding the third-party promo code → Critical Error (Misleading Information).

Do NOT apply this rule if:
- The conversation does not mention any promo code, voucher, or discount code.
- The promo code is only mentioned in passing without the flyer requesting assistance with it.
detection_checks: Detect promo code, voucher, or discount code mentions in the conversation
Verify agent identified the promo code source (Corendon vs third-party)
Check agent did not approve or deny promo code without Tier 3 verification
Verify escalation to Tier 3 for Corendon-issued promo codes
Verify agent directed flyer to issuing platform for third-party promo codes
Check for unauthorized commitments regarding promo code validity or eligibility

rules.description:
CUSTOMER ISSUE IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — ALL CATEGORIES)

For EVERY conversation, the agent must correctly identify the customer's primary issue before providing any assistance. The agent must understand what the customer actually needs — not what the ticket category says — and respond to that specific issue.

Violation Classification: Incorrect Issue Identification
Severity: Critical

Issue Types to Detect (not exhaustive):
Refund, Cancellation, Reschedule, Lost Baggage, Damaged Baggage, Promo Code, Check-in, Booking, Payment, Connecting Flight, Flight Delay, Compensation, Seat, Meal, Special Assistance, or any other customer request.

QA Failure Conditions:
- Agent misidentifies the customer's primary issue and responds to a different issue entirely.
- Agent ignores the customer's stated issue and provides assistance for an unrelated topic.
- Agent addresses a secondary issue while ignoring the primary issue the customer raised.
- Agent provides guidance for the wrong issue type (e.g., customer asks about a refund but agent responds with cancellation guidance).

Mandatory Report Fields on Failure:
1. The customer's actual primary issue (as stated in their own words — direct quote from the conversation).
2. The issue the agent incorrectly identified or responded to (with exact agent quote as evidence).
3. The exact chat evidence showing the mismatch between what the customer asked and what the agent addressed.
4. Explanation of the mismatch: why the agent's response does not address the customer's actual issue.
5. Expected handling: what the agent should have done to correctly identify and address the customer's actual issue.

Do NOT fail if:
- The agent correctly identified the primary issue even if they also addressed secondary issues.
- The agent asked a clarifying question to confirm the issue before responding — this is correct behaviour.
- The issue identification was correct but the resolution was incomplete — this is a separate finding (resolution quality), not an issue identification failure.
- The customer's issue evolved during the conversation and the agent adapted correctly.
- There is genuine ambiguity about the customer's primary issue and the agent asked for clarification.
detection_checks: STEP 1: Identify the customer's PRIMARY issue from their own words — ignore the ticket category label

STEP 1: Classify the issue type: Refund / Cancellation / Reschedule / Lost Baggage / Damaged Baggage / Promo Code / Check-in / Booking / Payment / Connecting Flight / Flight Delay / Compensation / Seat / Meal / Special Assistance / Other
STEP 2: Verify the agent's first substantive response addresses the correct issue type
STEP 2: If agent responds to a different issue type than what the customer stated → Critical Error (Incorrect Issue Identification)
STEP 3: Check whether the agent's guidance throughout the conversation remains aligned with the customer's actual issue
STEP 3: If agent drifts to a different issue or ignores the primary issue → Critical Error
For every violation: quote the customer's exact statement of their issue AND the agent's incorrect response as evidence
For every violation: explain the specific mismatch between customer issue and agent response
Do NOT fail if agent asked a clarifying question to confirm the issue — this is correct behaviour
Do NOT fail if issue identification was correct but resolution quality was poor — that is a separate finding

rules.description:
PROMO CODE HANDLING POLICY (MANDATORY — APPLIES WHENEVER A PROMO CODE, VOUCHER, OR DISCOUNT CODE IS MENTIONED)

This rule activates whenever the conversation involves a promo code, voucher, discount code, or promotional offer. The agent MUST perform ALL of the following steps that are applicable to the situation. Evaluate each step independently and generate a separate finding for each step that was violated or skipped.

--- MANDATORY VALIDATION CHECKLIST ---

STEP 1 — PROMO CODE SOURCE VERIFICATION (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
The agent MUST determine where the flyer obtained the promo code BEFORE providing any guidance:
- Was it issued by Corendon Airlines directly (via the Corendon website, app, email campaign, or official Corendon promotion)?
- Or was it issued by a third-party platform (travel agency, OTA, booking partner, or external website)?

QA Failure Condition:
- Agent provides ANY guidance about the promo code without first verifying its source → Missing Mandatory Information (Major Error).
- Agent assumes the promo code source without asking → Missing Mandatory Information (Major Error).

Evidence Required: Quote the exact agent message where guidance was given without source verification.

STEP 2 — IF THE PROMO CODE WAS ISSUED BY CORENDON AIRLINES:
The agent MUST:
- NOT approve, deny, confirm validity, or confirm eligibility of the promo code.
- NOT confirm whether the discount has been applied or will be applied.
- Collect the following before escalating: booking reference (if available), registered email address, the promo code itself, screenshot of the error or promotion (if applicable), description of the issue.
- Escalate the case to Tier 3 Support for verification and resolution.

QA Failure Conditions:
- Agent approves or denies the promo code without Tier 3 verification → Critical Error (Misleading Information / Unauthorized Commitment).
- Agent confirms the promo code is valid, invalid, eligible, or applicable without Tier 3 verification → Critical Error (Misleading Information).
- Agent confirms the discount has been or will be applied without Tier 3 verification → Critical Error (Misleading Information).
- Agent fails to escalate a Corendon-issued promo code case to Tier 3 → Major Error (Escalation Failure).
- Agent resolves the promo code issue without Tier 3 verification → Critical Error.

Evidence Required: Quote the exact agent message containing the unauthorized commitment or missing escalation.

STEP 3 — IF THE PROMO CODE WAS ISSUED BY A THIRD-PARTY PLATFORM:
The agent MUST:
- Advise the flyer to contact the platform that issued the promo code directly for further support.
- NOT attempt to verify, apply, override, or make any commitment about the third-party promo code.

QA Failure Conditions:
- Agent attempts to verify or apply a third-party promo code → Critical Error (Unauthorized Action / Misleading Information).
- Agent fails to direct the flyer to the issuing platform → Major Error (Incorrect Guidance).
- Agent makes any commitment regarding the third-party promo code validity, eligibility, or application → Critical Error (Misleading Information).

Evidence Required: Quote the exact agent message containing the unauthorized action or missing redirection.

--- PASS CONDITIONS ---
The agent PASSES this rule if:
- The promo code source was verified before any guidance was provided.
- For Corendon-issued codes: agent collected required information and escalated to Tier 3 without making any commitment.
- For third-party codes: agent correctly directed the flyer to the issuing platform without attempting to verify or apply the code.

--- DO NOT APPLY THIS RULE IF ---
- The conversation does not mention any promo code, voucher, discount code, or promotional offer.
- The promo code is only mentioned in passing without the flyer requesting any assistance with it.
detection_checks: STEP 1: Detect any mention of promo code, voucher, discount code, or promotional offer in the conversation
STEP 1: Verify the agent asked where the flyer obtained the promo code (Corendon vs third-party) BEFORE providing any guidance
STEP 1: If agent provided guidance without verifying source → Missing Mandatory Information (Major Error) — quote the exact message
STEP 2 (Corendon-issued): Verify agent did NOT approve, deny, or confirm promo code validity without Tier 3 verification
STEP 2 (Corendon-issued): Verify agent collected required information (booking ref, email, promo code, description) before escalating
STEP 2 (Corendon-issued): Verify agent escalated the case to Tier 3 Support — if not escalated → Major Error (Escalation Failure)
STEP 2 (Corendon-issued): If agent made any commitment about promo code without Tier 3 → Critical Error — quote the exact message
STEP 3 (Third-party): Verify agent directed flyer to the issuing platform — if not directed → Major Error (Incorrect Guidance)
STEP 3 (Third-party): Verify agent did NOT attempt to verify, apply, or make commitments about the third-party promo code
STEP 3 (Third-party): If agent made any commitment about third-party promo code → Critical Error — quote the exact message
For every violation found: include the exact chat evidence (direct quote) in the finding explanation

rules.description:
CALL SUPPORT ESCALATION POLICY (MANDATORY — ALL CATEGORIES)

This rule is MANDATORY and must be evaluated on EVERY conversation where escalation is applicable, regardless of category. It activates whenever the agent's response involves directing the flyer to an external contact channel for resolution of an issue that requires internal escalation.

--- POLICY ---

The correct internal escalation flow is:
  Tier 1 Agent (L1) → escalates internally to Tier 2 (L2)
  Tier 2 (L2) → escalates internally to Tier 3 / Dev (L3) when backend verification or back-office action is required

The agent MUST NEVER instruct the flyer to:
- Call customer support or a customer service phone number to resolve the issue.
- Contact a call centre directly for resolution of an issue that should be handled through internal escalation.
- Reach out to any external support channel as a substitute for creating an internal escalation.

The responsibility for internal escalation belongs entirely to the agent. The agent must NEVER transfer this responsibility to the flyer.

--- MANDATORY EVALUATION CHECKLIST ---

CHECK 1 — DETECT CALL SUPPORT REDIRECTION
Scan every agent message for any instruction that directs the flyer to call customer support, call a phone number, or contact a call centre as the resolution path for an issue that requires internal escalation.

Trigger phrases to detect (not exhaustive):
- please call our customer support
- please contact our call centre
- you can reach us by phone
- please call us at
- contact customer service directly
- call our support line
- please call support
- you will need to call
- I recommend you call
- Any instruction to phone Corendon Airlines support as the primary resolution path

QA Failure Condition:
- Agent instructs the flyer to call customer support or a call centre instead of creating an internal escalation → Critical Error (Incorrect Escalation Process).
- Evidence Required: Quote the exact agent message containing the call support instruction.

CHECK 2 — VERIFY INTERNAL ESCALATION WAS PERFORMED
When the conversation requires further investigation, backend verification, or resolution beyond L1 scope, verify that the agent created or offered an internal escalation (to Tier 2 or Tier 3) rather than redirecting the flyer externally.

QA Failure Condition:
- Agent closed or ended the interaction by redirecting the flyer to call support without creating an internal escalation → Critical Error (Incorrect Escalation Process).
- Evidence Required: Quote the exact agent message showing the external redirection and the absence of any internal escalation.

CHECK 3 — ANTI-FALSE-POSITIVE GATE
Do NOT fail this rule if:
- The agent correctly escalated internally AND also mentioned a phone number as an additional optional contact method (not as the sole resolution path).
- The issue was fully resolved at L1 level without requiring escalation.
- The agent directed the flyer to a third-party booking partner (not Corendon call support) for a third-party booking issue — this is correct behaviour per booking source policy.
- The conversation does not involve any escalation scenario at all.
- The agent mentioned call support only as a general information reference, not as the resolution instruction for the specific issue.

--- QA FAILURE CLASSIFICATION ---
Violation Name: Incorrect Escalation Process
Severity: Critical
Error Type: CRITICAL

Mandatory Report Fields on Failure:
1. The exact agent message containing the call support instruction (direct quote).
2. Explanation of why this violates the escalation policy: the agent transferred internal escalation responsibility to the flyer instead of creating an internal Tier 2 escalation.
3. Expected handling: the agent should have created an internal escalation to Tier 2, who would then escalate to Tier 3 / Dev if backend verification was required.
4. Customer impact: the flyer was incorrectly burdened with the responsibility of initiating a resolution process that should have been handled internally by the agent.

--- PASS CONDITIONS ---
The agent PASSES this rule if:
- The issue was resolved at Tier 1 level without requiring escalation, OR
- The agent created an internal escalation to Tier 2 / Tier 3 without instructing the flyer to call support as the resolution path, OR
- The conversation does not involve any scenario requiring internal escalation.
detection_checks: CHECK 1: Scan ALL agent messages for any instruction directing the flyer to call customer support, call a phone number, or contact a call centre as the resolution path

CHECK 1: Trigger phrases: 'please call our customer support', 'please contact our call centre', 'call us at', 'contact customer service directly', 'call our support line', 'please call support', 'you will need to call', 'I recommend you call'
CHECK 1: If any such instruction is found AND the issue required internal escalation → Critical Error (Incorrect Escalation Process) — quote the exact agent message
CHECK 2: Verify that when escalation was required, the agent created or offered an internal escalation (Tier 2 / Tier 3) rather than redirecting the flyer externally
CHECK 2: If agent closed the interaction by redirecting to call support without internal escalation → Critical Error — quote the exact message
CHECK 3 (Anti-false-positive): Do NOT fail if the issue was fully resolved at Tier 1 without escalation
CHECK 3 (Anti-false-positive): Do NOT fail if the agent directed the flyer to a third-party booking partner (not Corendon call support) for a third-party booking issue
CHECK 3 (Anti-false-positive): Do NOT fail if call support was mentioned only as an optional additional contact, not as the sole resolution path
For every violation: include the exact chat evidence (direct quote of the agent message) in the finding explanation
For every violation: explain why the guidance violates the escalation policy and what the correct handling should have been

rules.description:
3. Booking, Ticketing & Payments

• Payment Verification Issues

We are seeing cases where money is deducted from a customer's account (sometimes twice) without a booking confirmation being generated. What is the exact workflow to verify the payment, issue the ticket, or process the refund?

• Name Change Fees

Customers escalate when faced with a 300–400 Euro fee for a major name correction. How should we justify this charge to the customer, and are there any exception criteria we can apply?

• Reschedule Fee Breakdowns

When customers request a detailed breakdown of rescheduling charges, what information or system view should we use to explain the exact cost breakdown to them?

Response: Booking, Ticketing & Payments

1. Payment Verification Issues

Q: We are seeing cases where money is deducted from a customer's account (sometimes twice) without a booking confirmation being generated. What is the exact workflow to verify the payment, issue the ticket, or process the refund?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify payment status, duplicate transactions, ticket issuance, or refund processing.
• Collect the following details from the customer:
o Booking reference (if available)
o Passenger name
o Travel date
o Payment date and amount
o Payment method
o Transaction ID/Authorization ID
o Proof of payment (bank statement or payment receipt), if available
• Do not confirm that payment has been received or that a refund or ticket will be issued.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification with the Finance/Reservations Team.
• L3 will verify whether:
o The payment was successfully received.
o A booking or ticket exists.
o A duplicate payment was processed.
o A ticket can be issued or a refund is applicable.
• Document all relevant details in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
1. Cancellation / Refund / Reschedule Requests
Expected Agent Action:
The agent must first verify the actual booking source.
If the booking was made through a third-party travel agency, OTA, or booking partner, the agent must advise the customer to contact the respective booking partner directly for cancellation, refund, or rescheduling requests.
If the booking was made through the Corendon website or mobile application, the agent should guide the customer according to the applicable cancellation, refund, or rescheduling procedure.
QC Failure Conditions:
Agent provides cancellation/refund/reschedule assistance without verifying the booking source.
Agent gives incorrect information regarding eligibility or process.
Agent advises direct processing for third-party bookings.
Severity: Major
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. Reschedule Fee Breakdowns

Q: When customers request a detailed breakdown of rescheduling charges, what information should agents provide?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to the internal fare calculation or pricing engine used to determine rescheduling costs.
• Do not estimate or manually calculate the fare difference or change fees.
• If a customer requests a detailed breakdown of the rescheduling charges, collect the booking reference and details of the requested itinerary change.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification.
• L3 will review the booking, verify the applicable fare rules, airline change fee, fare difference, taxes, and any additional service charges, and provide the official breakdown to the customer.
• Record all customer requests and booking details in the CRM before escalating
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
Reschedule Fee Breakdowns

Q: When customers request a detailed breakdown of rescheduling charges, what information should agents provide?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to the internal fare calculation or pricing engine used to determine rescheduling costs.
• Do not estimate or manually calculate the fare difference or change fees.
• If a customer requests a detailed breakdown of the rescheduling charges, collect the booking reference and details of the requested itinerary change.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification.
• L3 will review the booking, verify the applicable fare rules, airline change fee, fare difference, taxes, and any additional service charges, and provide the official breakdown to the customer.
• Record all customer requests and booking details in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
## QA Rule – Payment Verification Issues

### Purpose

Evaluate whether the support agent correctly handled payment verification, ticket issuance, and refund-related queries according to their access level and responsibilities.

---

### Agent Access Limitations

L1 and L2 agents **do not have backend access** to:

* Verify payment status.
* Confirm duplicate transactions.
* Verify ticket issuance.
* Approve or confirm refunds.
* Access Finance or Reservation backend systems.

Only **L3 Support (Development/Finance/Reservations Team)** is authorized to perform these verifications.

---

### Required Information Gathering

Before escalating, the agent should collect the following information whenever applicable:

* Booking reference (if available)
* Passenger full name
* Travel date
* Payment date
* Payment amount
* Payment method
* Transaction ID / Authorization ID
* Proof of payment (bank statement, payment receipt, or screenshot), if available

Failure to gather essential information should be recorded as a QA observation.

---

### Correct Agent Behavior

The agent should:

* Acknowledge the customer's concern.
* Gather all relevant information.
* Explain that payment, ticket, or refund verification requires backend validation.
* Escalate the case when verification cannot be performed at their support level.
* Record all relevant details in the CRM before escalation.

---

### Escalation Process

The expected escalation flow is:

* **L1 Agent** → Escalate to **L2** if unable to resolve the issue.
* **L2 Agent** → Escalate to **L3 Support** if backend verification is required or the issue remains unresolved.
* **L3 Support (Development/Finance/Reservations Team)** is responsible for verifying:

  * Payment receipt
  * Booking existence
  * Ticket issuance
  * Duplicate transactions
  * Refund eligibility

---

### Critical Errors

Mark as a **Critical Error** if the agent, without backend verification:

* Confirms that payment has been received.
* Confirms that payment failed.
* Confirms that a duplicate payment occurred.
* Confirms that a booking exists.
* Confirms that a ticket has been issued.
* Confirms that a refund has been approved.
* Promises that a refund will be processed.
* Provides a refund processing timeframe.
* Gives any final resolution that requires backend verification.
* Assures the customer that the issue has been resolved without verification.

These actions exceed the authority of L1 and L2 agents and must be treated as misleading commitments.

---

### QA Evaluation Criteria

When reviewing the conversation, determine whether the agent:

* Gathered all necessary information before responding.
* Stayed within the permissions of their support level.
* Avoided making unverified commitments.
* Escalated the issue appropriately when unable to verify or resolve it.
* Documented the case correctly before escalation.

### QA Decision

An agent **passes** this QA check if they:

* Follow the required troubleshooting process.
* Collect the necessary customer information.
* Avoid making unsupported commitments.
* Escalate the issue according to the support hierarchy.

An agent **fails** this QA check if they:

* Skip essential information gathering.
* Provide unverified confirmations or promises.
* Fail to escalate when required.
* Resolve or close the issue without the necessary backend verification.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
Expected Agent Action:
The agent must first verify the actual booking source.
If the booking was made through a third-party travel agency, OTA, or booking partner, the agent must advise the customer to contact the respective booking partner directly for cancellation, refund, or rescheduling requests.
If the booking was made through the Corendon website or mobile application, the agent should guide the customer according to the applicable cancellation, refund, or rescheduling procedure.
QC Failure Conditions:
Agent provides cancellation/refund/reschedule assistance without verifying the booking source.
Agent gives incorrect information regarding eligibility or process.
Agent advises direct processing for third-party bookings.
Severity: Major
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
• Missing Personal Items

If a customer leaves an item (e.g., laptop, jacket) on the plane and contacts Lost & Found, they are sometimes assured they will get it back, but no formal PIR or tracking number is created. How do we address and track these cases?

Response: Missing Personal Items (Left on Board)

If a customer reports that they have left a personal item (e.g., a laptop, mobile phone, jacket, passport, or other belongings) on board the aircraft, inform them that Corendon Airlines does not issue a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) or tracking number for personal items left on the aircraft, as a PIR is only applicable to checked baggage.

Advise the customer that their request will be handled through the Lost & Found process. If the item is located during the aircraft cleaning or handling process, the relevant airport's Lost & Found office or the responsible ground handling agent will contact the customer with further instructions for collection or shipment, where applicable.

If the customer states that they were assured they would receive the item back but have not received any update, advise them to follow up directly with the Lost & Found office at the arrival airport. Corendon Airlines is unable to provide a tracking number or live status updates for personal belongings left on board.

Agents should document the case in the CRM by marking it as "Personal Item Left on Board – Referred to Lost & Found" and include the item description, flight number, travel date, arrival airport, and the customer's contact details in the case notes.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

If the item is located during the aircraft cleaning or handling process, the relevant airport's Lost & Found office or the responsible ground handling agent will contact the customer with further instructions for collection or shipment, where applicable.

Corendon Airlines is unable to provide a tracking number or live status updates for personal belongings left on board.

Agents should document the case in the CRM by marking it as "Personal Item Left on Board – Referred to Lost & Found" and include the item description, flight number, travel date, arrival airport, and the customer's contact details in the case notes.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
MANDATORY BAGGAGE GUIDANCE CHECKLIST — Lost / Missing / Damaged Baggage

This rule applies whenever the customer reports lost baggage, missing baggage, or damaged baggage.

The agent MUST provide ALL of the following guidance points that are applicable to the customer's situation. Failure to cover any applicable point is a QA violation classified as Missing Mandatory Information / Critical Guidance.

Mandatory Checklist (evaluate each point independently):

1. PIR Requirement at Airport
The agent must inform the flyer that a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) must be completed at the airport (at the Lost & Found / Baggage Service Desk in the arrivals hall) before leaving the airport. This is mandatory to initiate any baggage tracing, claim, or compensation process.

2. PIR Cannot Be Submitted Online
The agent must explicitly inform the flyer that a PIR cannot be submitted online for Corendon Airlines. The PIR must be filed in person at the airport. Do not imply or suggest that an online submission is possible.

3. Flyer Has Already Left the Airport
If the flyer states or implies that they have already left the airport without filing a PIR, the agent must advise them to return to the airport and complete the PIR as soon as possible. The agent must not simply accept that the PIR was missed without advising the flyer to return.

4. Connecting Flight Verification
The agent must verify whether the flyer's itinerary includes a connecting flight. This is mandatory before providing any baggage transfer guidance.

5. All-Corendon Connection — Baggage Transfer
If all connecting flights in the itinerary are operated by Corendon Airlines, the agent must explain that baggage is normally transferred automatically to the final destination and the flyer does not need to collect and re-check it at the connecting airport.

6. Mixed-Airline Connection — Baggage Re-Check
If the connection involves different airlines (i.e., not all flights are operated by Corendon Airlines), the agent must explain that the flyer is required to collect their baggage at the connecting airport and re-check it for the next flight. Failure to inform the flyer of this requirement is a Critical Guidance failure.

QA Failure Conditions:
- Agent fails to mention the PIR requirement → Major Error.
- Agent implies or states that PIR can be submitted online → Critical Error (Misleading Information).
- Agent does not advise the flyer to return to the airport when they have already left without filing a PIR → Major Error.
- Agent does not verify whether a connecting flight is involved before providing baggage transfer guidance → Major Error.
- Agent provides incorrect baggage transfer guidance for mixed-airline connections → Critical Error (Misleading Information).
- Agent fails to explain the re-check requirement for mixed-airline connections → Critical Error (Missing Mandatory Guidance).

Severity: Critical / Major (depending on the specific point missed — see above)
detection_checks: Verify all 6 mandatory baggage guidance points are covered
Check PIR guidance accuracy
Verify connecting flight baggage transfer guidance
Compare customer situation with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
2. Flight Disruptions & Compensation (IRROPS)

• Delay Verification

Customers frequently escalate when we cannot verify the exact length and reason for a flight delay. What internal tool or process should we use to accurately verify delay reasons and durations?

Response:

1. Delay Verification

Q: Customers frequently escalate when we cannot verify the exact length and reason for a flight delay. What internal tool or process should we use to accurately verify delay reasons and durations?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to operational systems to verify the exact flight delay duration or the official reason for the disruption.
• Do not speculate or communicate unverified information to the customer.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification with the Operations Team/OCC.
• L3 will verify the official delay duration and reason and provide the appropriate response to the customer.
• Record all customer details, flight number, travel date, and the specific information requested before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
2. Missed Connections

Q: How should we handle customers who miss a Corendon flight because their previous (separate or connected) flight was delayed?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents should not determine liability or eligibility for rebooking or compensation.
• Collect the booking reference(s), flight details, and customer explanation.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification of the booking type (separate booking or protected connection) and applicable airline policy.
• L3 will review the itinerary and provide the final resolution based on Corendon Airlines' procedures.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. EU261 & Hotel Accommodation

Q: Customers frequently cite EU261 laws to demand hotel stays and compensation. What are the specific guidelines and approval flows for granting or denying hotel accommodations and compensation?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents are not authorized to approve or deny hotel accommodation, reimbursement, or EU261 compensation.
• Do not promise eligibility, compensation amounts, or reimbursement.
• If the customer requests hotel accommodation, expense reimbursement, or compensation, escalate the case to L3 Support for review.
• L3 will verify the operational circumstances, determine eligibility under Corendon Airlines' policy and applicable passenger rights regulations, and provide the final response or guide the customer through the official claim process.

 
4. Tarmac Delays

Q: When a flight is stuck on the tarmac, customers often contact us stating that ground or cabin crews are not providing updates. How should we handle these live escalations and provide accurate departure estimates?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to live operational updates or estimated departure times.
• Do not provide estimated departure times or operational information unless officially confirmed.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification with the Operations Team, where applicable.
• If no official information is available, advise the customer that the flight crew and airport operations will make announcements as soon as confirmed information becomes available.
• Document the interaction in the CRM before escalating the case.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. EU261 & Hotel Accommodation

detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. Reschedule Fee Breakdowns

Q: When customers request a detailed breakdown of rescheduling charges, what information should agents provide?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to the internal fare calculation or pricing engine used to determine rescheduling costs.
• Do not estimate or manually calculate the fare difference or change fees.
• If a customer requests a detailed breakdown of the rescheduling charges, collect the booking reference and details of the requested itinerary change.
• Escalate the case to L3 Support for verification.
• L3 will review the booking, verify the applicable fare rules, airline change fee, fare difference, taxes, and any additional service charges, and provide the official breakdown to the customer.
• Record all customer requests and booking details in the CRM before escalating
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
2. Name Change Fees

Q: Customers escalate when faced with a high fee for a major name correction. How should we justify this charge, and are there any exception criteria?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents should explain that name correction or name change fees are determined according to Corendon Airlines' fare conditions and ticket rules.
• Do not negotiate, waive, or promise any reduction in the applicable fee.
• If the customer claims the correction is due to a booking error, legal name change, or another exceptional circumstance, collect all supporting documents (where applicable) and escalate the case to L3 Support.
• L3 will verify whether the request qualifies for an exception under Corendon Airlines' policy and communicate the final decision.
• Ensure all supporting documents and customer comments are attached to the CRM case before escalation.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
1. Severe Allergies

Q: What is the protocol for handling sensitive escalations where a customer was served food they are allergic to or suffered an allergic reaction onboard?

Guideline:

• Treat all allergy-related complaints as high-priority safety incidents.
• L1 and L2 agents should express empathy and collect all relevant details, including:
o Booking reference
o Flight number and travel date
o Description of the allergy
o Food or beverage consumed
o Symptoms experienced
o Whether medical assistance was provided onboard or after landing
o Any supporting medical documents (if available)
• Do not determine liability, admit fault, or promise compensation.
• Escalate the case immediately to L3 Support for review with the In-Flight Services/Safety Team.
• L3 will verify the incident, coordinate with the relevant operational departments, and provide the official response regarding the complaint and any applicable resolution.
• Document all information in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
2. Pre-Ordered Meal Discrepancies

Q: Customers present a pre-order meal confirmation, but the cabin crew advised that no meal request was available. How should this be handled?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents should request:
o Booking reference
o Flight number and travel date
o Copy of the meal confirmation or payment receipt
o Type of meal ordered
• Do not confirm whether the meal request was correctly processed or whether compensation will be provided.
• As L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify meal synchronization between the reservation system and onboard catering, the case must be escalated to L3 Support.
• L3 will verify whether the meal was successfully added to the booking, transmitted to the catering provider, or affected by a system synchronization issue.
• If applicable, L3 will determine whether reimbursement or any goodwill compensation is appropriate according to Corendon Airlines' policy.
• Record all supporting documents and customer comments in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. Onboard Wi-Fi

Q: We need clear documentation on how the Corendon Wi-Fi facility works, including purchasing, troubleshooting, and refunding failed connections.

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents should first determine:
o Flight number and travel date
o Whether the aircraft was equipped with Wi-Fi
o Whether the customer purchased the Wi-Fi service onboard or in advance
o The nature of the issue (unable to connect, slow connection, interrupted service, payment issue, etc.)
o Any proof of purchase or payment receipt
• Provide only basic troubleshooting, such as:
o Ensuring the device is in Flight Mode with Wi-Fi enabled.
o Confirming the customer connected to the aircraft's Wi-Fi network.
o Advising the customer to reconnect or restart the device if appropriate.
• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify Wi-Fi service availability, technical failures, or refund eligibility.
• If the issue cannot be resolved or the customer requests a refund, escalate the case to L3 Support.
• L3 will verify the Wi-Fi service logs, confirm whether a service disruption occurred, determine refund eligibility according to Corendon Airlines' policy, and provide the final response.
• Ensure all customer details, proof of purchase, and issue description are documented in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
5. Loyalty & Airport Operations

• Miles & Loyalty Programs

Our team has not received training on Corendon's Miles, Promo Codes, or Loyalty Program. Handling these queries currently leads to immediate escalations. Can you provide training materials or an SOP for loyalty-related issues?

• Boarding Pass Scanning Errors

When a boarding pass fails to scan at the gate, ground staff sometimes refuse to help and direct the customer to call us. What action can we take remotely to resolve a live gate-scanning issue?

Response: Membership, Loyalty Program & Promo Codes

Q: Our team has not received training on Corendon's Membership, Promo Codes, or Loyalty Program. Handling these queries currently leads to immediate escalations. Can you provide an SOP?

Guideline:

• L1 and L2 agents must first verify the original booking source, as promotional codes, loyalty benefits, or discounts may vary depending on where the booking was made.
• Confirm whether the booking was made:
o Directly through Corendon Airlines (website, mobile app, or Customer Service).
o Through a travel agency, tour operator, OTA (Online Travel Agency), or any third-party vendor.
• Some third-party agencies and travel partners operate their own loyalty programs, promotional campaigns, voucher codes, or reward schemes, which are independent of Corendon Airlines. These offers cannot be verified or managed by L1/L2 Customer Support.
• If the promotion, voucher, or loyalty benefit was issued by a third-party booking partner, advise the customer to contact the respective booking provider, as they are responsible for validating and administering their own promotional offers.
• If the booking was made directly with Corendon Airlines and the issue relates to a Corendon promotional campaign, membership account, or promo code, collect:
o Booking reference
o Registered email address
o Promo code (if applicable)
o Screenshot of the error or promotion
o Description of the issue
• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify:
o Membership account status
o Promo code eligibility
o Campaign participation
o Discount application failures
• Do not manually apply promo codes, override discounts, or promise exceptions.
• Escalate eligible Corendon-related cases to L3 Support for verification. L3 will confirm the booking source, campaign eligibility, account status, and provide the appropriate resolution.
• Ensure all customer information and supporting documents are recorded in the CRM before escalating.

 
Important Guidelines for L1/L2 Agents Before Escalation

Mandatory Information Gathering

Before escalating any case to L3 Support, L1 and L2 agents must clearly understand the customer's actual issue and collect all relevant information required for investigation. The quality of the escalation depends on the accuracy and completeness of the information provided.

Agent Responsibilities

• Understand the customer's concern before initiating an escalation.
• Ask only relevant and case-specific questions required to investigate the issue.
• Verify all available details within the scope of L1/L2 support.
• Collect all mandatory information (e.g., booking reference, passenger name, flight number, travel date, etc...) based on the issue type.
• Information a clear summary of the customer's concern and all collected information in the CRM before escalating the case.

Critical Compliance
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

5. Loyalty & Airport Operations

• Miles & Loyalty Programs

• If the promotion, voucher, or loyalty benefit was issued by a third-party booking partner, advise the customer to contact the respective booking provider, as they are responsible for validating and administering their own promotional offers.
• If the booking was made directly with Corendon Airlines and the issue relates to a Corendon promotional campaign, membership account, or promo code, collect:
o Booking reference
o Registered email address
o Promo code (if applicable)
o Screenshot of the error or promotion
o Description of the issue
• L1 and L2 agents do not have access to verify:
o Membership account status
o Promo code eligibility
o Campaign participation
o Discount application failures
• Do not manually apply promo codes, override discounts, or promise exceptions.
• Escalate eligible Corendon-related cases to L3 Support for verification. L3 will confirm the booking source, campaign eligibility, account status, and provide the appropriate resolution.
• Ensure all customer information and supporting documents are recorded in the CRM before escalating.

 
Important Guidelines for L1/L2 Agents Before Escalation

Mandatory Information Gathering

Before escalating any case to L3 Support, L1 and L2 agents must clearly understand the customer's actual issue and collect all relevant information required for investigation. The quality of the escalation depends on the accuracy and completeness of the information provided.

Agent Responsibilities

• Understand the customer's concern before initiating an escalation.
• Ask only relevant and case-specific questions required to investigate the issue.
• Verify all available details within the scope of L1/L2 support.
• Collect all mandatory information (e.g., booking reference, passenger name, flight number, travel date, etc...) based on the issue type.
• Information a clear summary of the customer's concern and all collected information in the CRM before escalating the case.

Critical Compliance

• Agents must not ask customers for unnecessary or irrelevant information.
• If an L1/L2 agent requests information that is not required for handling or investigating the reported issue, the interaction should be marked as a Critical Error during QA evaluation.
• Asking irrelevant questions increases customer effort, delays resolution, and negatively impacts the customer experience.

Escalation Standard

A case should be escalated to L3 only after:

The actual issue has been clearly identified.
All mandatory information has been collected.
Relevant supporting information have been obtained (where applicable).
The CRM notes contain a complete and accurate summary of the issue.
The agent has confirmed that the request cannot be resolved within the scope of L1/L2 responsibilities.
Note: Incomplete escalations or escalations with missing mandatory information may be returned by L3 for additional details, resulting in unnecessary delays in resolution.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
• Onboard Wi-Fi

We need clear documentation on how the Corendon Wi-Fi facility works, including purchasing, troubleshooting, and refunding failed connections.

Response: In-Flight Services & Experience

1. Severe Allergies

Q: What is the protocol for handling sensitive escalations where a customer was served food they are allergic to or suffered an allergic reaction onboard?

Guideline:

• Treat all allergy-related complaints as high-priority safety incidents.
• L1 and L2 agents should express empathy and collect all relevant details, including:
o Booking reference
o Flight number and travel date
o Description of the allergy
o Food or beverage consumed
o Symptoms experienced
o Whether medical assistance was provided onboard or after landing
o Any supporting medical documents (if available)
• Do not determine liability, admit fault, or promise compensation.

• Document all information in the CRM before escalating.
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy

rules.description:
3. PNR / Booking Verification Requests
Expected Agent Action:
Agents do not have access to verify PNRs, booking ownership, passenger details, or booking authenticity.
All PNR and booking verification requests must be escalated to Tier 3 Support for further verification.
The agent should clearly inform the customer that the request has been escalated for verification.
QC Failure Conditions:
Agent attempts to verify a booking without authorized access.
Agent marks the issue as resolved without Tier 3 verification.
Agent provides booking-related confirmation without validation.
Severity: Critical / Misleading Information
Critical Error Definition:
Any case where an agent resolves a PNR or booking verification request without proper Tier 3 escalation and verification will be marked as a Critical Error (Misleading Information) and may result in immediate QA failure for the interaction.
Send to all QC members
detection_checks: Compare customer intent with agent response
Verify against official policy
